selected_topic: Deadly “red Sky” Solar Storm from 800 Years Ago Discovered in Ancient Trees
news_angle: Scientists uncovered evidence of a massive ancient solar storm, dubbed the 'red Sky' event, by analyzing tree rings, revealing powerful solar activity from 800 years ago that impacts our understanding of space weather risks.
why_this_is_news: This discovery sheds new light on historical solar storm events, helping scientists better predict future space weather hazards and their potential impact on Earth and technology.
specific_action: Discovered through analysis of tree rings
scene_metaphor: Hunk Mao in sleek space explorer gear navigating a cosmic forest of glowing, gigantic tree rings swirling with fiery red solar storm energy beams.

caption: Scientists found a massive solar storm that hit Earth 800 years ago by studying ancient trees. Hunk Mao says even old trees have wild stories—space danger lurks in every ring!
